reviews.title: I am disappointed and frustrated. I will never stay there again
reviews.positives: Location is good
reviews.negatives:
We arrived at 8 PM, but by the time we got there, our room had already been given away to someone else because they could make more money. We only found this out afterward. That night, they put the three of us in one room and told us we would get a better room the next day.

The situation was very uncomfortable. There were no blankets, and both of my friends couldn’t sleep. When we asked the front desk for help, they said they didn’t know where to find any blankets. Even though we didn’t get the room we reserved, they still required full payment upfront for the entire 10-day stay, which was extremely frustrating since we were already exhausted.

The next day, instead of giving us the presidential suite I had booked, they gave me a regular room and expected me to share a bed with my male friend. I had to argue and insist just to get the room I originally reserved.

Overall, I was very unhappy and disappointed with the experience. I didn’t want to stay there any longer, but my brother and sister-in-law preferred to stay because it was closer to her father’s house. I will never stay here again.

reviews.title: Affordable hotel for a family of 5 before departure (2 rooms, 0.5 nights)
reviews.text:
Since my parents are older, I chose a budget hotel where I can relax comfortably, even if it’s a bit lower tier than a busy station/lounge that requires a lot of attention.
- The soundproofing was better than expected (the same floor was under construction but we didn't hear it)
- Cleanliness and good facilities were below expectations
- A ridiculous value for money with a reservation for two rooms at around 70,000 won
- Good geographical location, close to the airport and the main attractions of Da Nang (Han Market, Pink Church, etc.)
- They allow you to leave your luggage before the 2 PM check-in, but it seems like they just put it in a room behind the front desk without taking any responsibility if someone steals it. (It might be essential to take photos of your luggage before leaving it.)
reviews.room_type: Superior Double Room with City View

reviews.title: Not satisfied.
reviews.positives: Nearby downtown
reviews.negatives: The towels are too thin and worn out. Need new towels. Water leaks and wet entire restroom after washing and showering. Reported to staff about water leaks issues but no effective solution nor acknowledgement. This is a concern for my family member who is an elder and I was concerned about slippery due to water on the floor in the restroom. Cups for coffees are too small. Overall, this hotel is overrated for 4 stars which is not good for the customers to believe in.

reviews.title: 0.5-night budget hotel (2 rooms) for a family of 5 before departure.
reviews.text:
I chose this budget hotel for my elderly parents, as it's a more comfortable place to rest, even if it's slightly lower in quality, compared to busier and more stressful stations/lounges.

- The soundproofing was better than expected (there was construction on the same floor, but I didn't hear anything).
- Cleanliness and good facilities were below expectations.
- Booking two rooms for about 70,000 won was an unbelievable value.
- Its geographical location is great, being close to the airport and main attractions in Da Nang (Han Market, Pink Church, etc.).
- You can leave your luggage before the 2 PM check-in, but they just put it behind the front desk, so I feel like they wouldn’t take responsibility if someone stole it. (It seems necessary to take a photo of your luggage before leaving it.)

reviews.text: I stayed here to watch the Fireworks festival and the room they put me in was so ugly and bad. First, the room dont have the lights all around and the toilet has only one light. It was so dim in the room during the night I ask the receptionist but she said that I dont need all the lights! 😠 Although, she said, she will send someone to fix but no one did. Wi-fi was not working as well when I came in. No freaking Telephone, so if you have an urgent concern. You have to go down to 1st floor and imagine you are in the higher floor. The inconvenience of this hotel is just too much, I never write bad reviews but this just push me to my limit and I checked out early morning the day after. Went to better accommodation.

reviews.title: Possible Bedbugs
reviews.positives: Good location.
reviews.negatives:
When checking my bed, I noticed a line of red dots on the sheets. This can be a sign of bedbugs feeding. The mattress itself looked fine, so I asked for them to change the sheets and stayed the night. I thought it was perhaps a sign of a past but not current infestation…

A few days later I noticed bed bug poop in my luggage and bed bugs bites on my back (bites can take a few days to appear). I cannot say with 100% certainty that they came from this hotel, but it is the only hotel I stayed at that had ANY signs of bedbugs. Additionally, I messaged every hotel I stayed at in Vietnam alerting them to check their rooms and every hotel but Moonlight Suites responded confirming they would check their rooms. It has been five weeks and the property has not responded to my message.

I do not recommend staying here and regret it heavily.

reviews.title: Bad experience and receptionist with that attitude
reviews.positives: The location is the only thing that I like about.
reviews.negatives:
Upon our arrival, we are asked to move to another hotel which is like 2 or 3* which was much downgraded from the hotel room we booked at Moonlight. I was told that our room has sth wrong with the aircon and we need to stay at that hotel for a night. We arrived so late and tired, we didn't say much and accepted it. But when we checked-in the next day, seems our room was occupied and the room was still cleaning in progress though we came back after our day tour. I guess, they double booked our room and asked us to move to other hotel in previous night which is not acceptable and not sincere at all. 

The next thing is, the hotel receptionist guy was rude when we asked for another room key since we are two people and he gave only one. He just asked with blank face and ask " why do you need another key?" not in a polite or professional way which a normal receptionist would do. 
Anyway overall except from the location itself, its not value for money at all with all the bad experiences that we had.

reviews.title: The hotel needs to improve its facilities as well as support services.
reviews.text:
Advantages: located in the city center.  
Disadvantages: The hotel was built a long time ago. Facilities are deteriorating. The bedding is old, and even moldy. The window latch is broken. The air conditioning system is broken. The staff checked and then stayed silent without changing the room or reporting back.  
Needs significant improvement.

reviews.title: Moldy rooms with no hot water
reviews.positives: The view was nice and location was decent
reviews.negatives:
First room looked nice, in good condition, at 15th floor, offering a nice view of city and Dragon Bridge. Unfortunately, there was no hot water....not even warm. Had to wait almost an hour for them to check that indeed to warm water was coming and to offer us a new room. 
Second room was in a much worse condition, having black mold and dampness in the walls. But it had warm water and it was the only one available. We only had warm water in the shower, none at the sink or in the bathtub.
Staff was not very helpful, not being able to speak English, but also not really using Google translate when we tried.
Lighting in rooms was very bad. Also there is big weird window connecting the bedroom to the bathroom. 
I do not recommend this hotel, especially since we paid extra for a nice suite with river view. Very disappointing.

reviews.title: They might overbook you, and read Google reviews before you book
reviews.positives: Close to the center
reviews.negatives: They overbooked us and didn’t let us know until we showed up at the hotel at 11pm. I’d also contacted them beforehand regarding bike rental and never heard back. They let us stay at a nearby hotel for the night and told us our room would be ready the next day. That other hotel was nasty, cockroaches running around, trash not taken out, wifi not working, etc. My partner read some more reviews for Moonlight on Google and it turned out that those were worse than the ones on Booking. We decided to look for another hotel the next morning because we didn’t want to gamble on another horrible stay after a day full of activities. Still had to pay the full price when we left even though they overbooked us since it’s booking.com’s policy. Also, a local taxi driver told us “Why would you stay at Moonlight” so there’s that

reviews.title: My stay
reviews.text: Disappointed in our stay here. It was supposed to be breakfast included. When we arrived to check in, we were advised breakfast was not available to us due to an issue. They did say they up graded our room, but given we had already booked  superior riverview room anyway,  I'm not sure the room we were given was an upgrade. There was no hot water for showers and the sofa was ripped. As for the lack of breakfast. This was an inconvenience and we had to spend a large part of the morning trying to locate somewhere to eat. We would have preferred to have been given the option to cancel the booking and found somewhere else with breakfast included, or given option to transfer to another hotel.

reviews.title: Would I stay again...no I would not.
reviews.positives: The location is close to restaurants and the river. Had an English movie channel. It was cheap and had ok views to the surrounding hills/across the city. They organised a taxi to the airport for us at 5am for a good price.
reviews.negatives: There is a strict 2pm check in - although it seemed empty and we arrived around 1pm. Although we booked a family room for 4 people, with 2x double beds there were only 2 pillows and 2 towels. The shower screen in the bathroom looked like it had never been cleaned, there is wall damage which suggests a big leak from the bathroom, the room smelt musty and we had to keep the window open. Photos are all AI and do not represent the view from the hotel AT ALL. The hotel is at the tail end of the dragon bridge. The upstairs "party" level was empty/storage area, no idea if gym is above this as we didn't feel comfortable walking through the upper floor to get to the gym level (as elevator didn't go that high).

reviews.negatives:

Teapot had some leftovers from the previous guests, coffee cups had brown stains, decorative pillows looked dirty, much as the windows.. The first night, I had an unexpected visitor: a cockroach.

reviews.title: Not for sensitive guests
reviews.positives:
While I can’t provide a comprehensive assessment given our brief stay, I’m still giving this accommodation five stars based on what we needed at the time. We only booked the room for a short rest and a shower before a late flight, and it delivered exactly that.

For a very reasonable rate, we received a room equipped with a hot tub, a spacious lavatory counter, a good beds, and a location that’s both central and just a 10-minute taxi ride from the airport. These conveniences more than justified the cost for a short stay.
reviews.negatives: It felt like a 40-50yr old hotel. It’s still “okay” but I believe it was much nicer decades ago. If you’re picky and planning a longer vacation and have more flexibility with your budget, I would recommend looking elsewhere. I wouldn’t suggest this property for stays longer than one night.

The room we ended up staying in had a both, but the fridge wasn’t in its cabinet & it turned off every time we left the room because of the key pocket electrical control function. Keeping things cold was impossible! Overall, we were willing to pay what we did for the space, but hopefully they have some renovations planned.

reviews.title: A filthy hotel with no hot water supply
reviews.text:
The room boasted a good view overlooking the Han River. However, upon checking in, I found a thin layer of dust covering the entire suite floor (and yes, it was a suite on a high floor). The paint on the walls was peeling, looking very much as though it hadn’t been maintained for quite some time.

The most unacceptable issue was the complete lack of hot water. The front desk staff tried running the water for over twenty minutes, but not a single drop of hot water came from either the shower or the taps. A very disappointing hotel experience indeed.

Extended my stay for another 2 weeks as Da Nang started to grow on me after a couple weeks exploring.  Be sure to walk, get lost and discover the city around & across the river.  I would highly recommend staying at Moonlight if on a budget or extended stay.  Hiring a cheap Grab bike or car makes everything accessible for only a few dollars.
reviews.negatives: No microwave or ability to warm up food.  Visited during rainy season so overcast or rained almost everyday.  Unable to swim in sea due to high under toe and waves.  A lot of street construction which made navigating the city by walking challenging.

reviews.title: It was fine
reviews.text:
The room I had was great. When I walked in I was impressed by the spacious room and high ceilings. I was quite happy, until I realized I was given a room with an adjoining room. Every night for the full duration, there were people in the adjoining room, separated by a door, who made a lot of noise. There was no soundproofing of any sort, just a door between rooms, with inches of space on the top and bottom to let you hear everything. I don't blame the neighbor residents, it was just not made to accommodate two separate parties with privacy.

Overall this was a good room but there needs to be a way to create some privacy if there are people in adjacent rooms.

reviews.title: Inappropriate room
reviews.text:
We were assigned a room that was not appropriate for a couple. The room we received was adjacent to another, resulting in a lack of privacy. We could hear the other family talking, and their child was quite loud, especially early in the morning, crying as if we were not in the room next to them. Our room is large, with an additional door that separates us from them. My husband and I had to whisper when speaking to avoid being overheard.

I kindly request that more consideration be given when assigning rooms for couples like us. Additionally, the water from the shower flows outside the bathroom, creating a messy situation after showering.

Thank you for your attention to these matters.

reviews.text: This is a very nicely designed hotel.The room I had on the 15th floor had fabulous views over DaNang.The room layout great.unfortunately a few things lets this hotel down and with improvement would be an excellent hotel.I think the cleaning wasn't up to standard.A bottle top was on the floor behind a chair quite easy to see when I first walked in the room and was still there when I left.The shower water was never really hot,just warm.At times there was a bad smell comming from the toilet or floor vent.I didn't see any door tags throughout the hotel the ones that say do not disturb or please make up the room .On the second morning I was having a shower and I assume it was a cleaning man walked straight into the room without knocking.Breakfast was average and mostly just warm,The 2 floors for massage were very dubious.Overall the staff very nice and I would certainly stay there again.Hopefully management can ma ke improvements.

Although English was not widely spoken, I was able to communicate using Google Translate and even borrowed a charging cable that had broken. The main tourist attractions were within walking distance, and it was about a 15-minute taxi ride from the airport. There were also many local Michelin-rated restaurants and cafes near the hotel, which made for an enjoyable experience.

On top of the great service, the prices are very affordable, making it a fantastic value for money. I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a comfortable, budget-friendly stay right in the heart of the city.
reviews.negatives: Although breakfast is not included, there are plenty of great dining options just around the hotel, making it very easy to find something delicious nearby.

reviews.positives:
Great location, right in the city centre, close to the Dragon Bridge, very easy to get around and everything you need is just a short walk away. Lots of great food options nearby which is a big plus. The room was extremely clean, tidy and beautifully appointed. For such a low price, I didn't expect it to be so nice. Definitely good value for money.
 One of the highlights was the view: from my room on a high floor, I had a stunning view of the city and the famous Dragon Bridge. Watching the city lights at night from the window was such a nice bonus.
The staff were friendly and always willing to help. Overall, I had a very comfortable and pleasant stay.
reviews.negatives: There’s no breakfast offered, which might be a downside for some, but with so many tasty and affordable local places right outside, it didn’t bother me at all.

Our room was clean, cozy, and had a stunning view of the city and the famous Dragon Bridge, especially beautiful at night when the lights came on.
